description_long: This object is a lifelike representation of a duck with a streamlined, elongated body and a slightly upturned tail. The plumage is predominantly gray with white patches on the sides and underbelly, and darker gray to black markings on the head and back. The head is rounded with a short, flat bill, and the wings are tucked closely against the body, giving it a smooth, compact appearance. The surface appears matte and slightly textured, resembling the look of carved or molded material rather than real feathers. The overall form is low to the ground, suggesting a resting or floating posture. The coloration and proportions are consistent with a small diving duck species.
description: A gray and white duck with a streamlined body, rounded head, and tucked wings, featuring realistic feather patterns and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the duck's elongated body, rounded head, and detailed feather pattern along the flank.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail, symmetrical body shape, and the subtle gradation of color from back to tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same streamlined profile and feather details as the first side.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the rounded head, short bill, and the symmetry of the body and wings.
